purpose: Small paraphrase robustness subset for judge/rubric stability, not answer-generation benchmarking.
selection_rule: Choose probes where wording ambiguity likely affects judge interpretation and where the selected human calibration subset already gives comparison coverage.

selected_probes.reason: Shortest, handle-poor current-state ask; tests referent ambiguity.
paraphrases: what is the current live state right now?
where should I pick things back up from here?
what is the latest active thread I should resume?

selected_probes.original: check it what happened in the last session don't forget it please
paraphrases: what happened in the immediately previous session?
recover the latest session state so we do not lose it
what should I remember from the last session before continuing?

selected_probes.reason: Most-recent-work ask is surface-agnostic and known to have target-slice tie risk.
selected_probes.original: what happened last? what was I working on most recently?
paraphrases: what was the latest thing I was working on?
what is the most recent work thread before this point?
what should I resume from the immediately prior work?

selected_probes.reason: Open-thread/priorities ask mixes completeness and subjective salience.
selected_probes.original: what are all my current open threads, priorities, and unfinished work? include: research vectors (ACE, GEPA, sleep/dream, filesystem), open bugs, hackathon plans, memory system studies, continual learning research
paraphrases: list the active unfinished threads and priorities, including research, bugs, hackathon work, memory studies, and continual learning
what work is still open right now across research vectors, bugs, plans, and memory-system studies?
map my current unfinished work and priorities without dropping the named research areas

planned_measurements: same-answer judge verdict movement under paraphrased ask text
run_rule: Do not run broad paraphrase experiments until the claim-audit template has been applied to the 30-cell calibration subset.
